rationale_by_leg.zai-glm-5p2: Broad, faithful cycle coverage: the HF/OpenAI breach plus Gemini 4 pre-training, DeepMind's three models incl. Flash Cyber, the GPT-6 briefing, US-China talks, xAI's 2T run, and the IMO-2026 solve — all verified in-snapshot (lines 850, 997, 9599, 17204, 15338). Correctly flags the GPT-6 identity as AndrewCurran's single-source inference and cites Clem's counter-framing and the WSJ regulatory-capture angle. Headlines are diverse and alert-worthy. Weaknesses: the marquee story is handled more shallowly than the report allows (never notes the report itself named GPT-5.6 Sol / ExploitGym), and it carries signal-only-contract violations — reader-facing operational filler about the 'run sandbox' blocking fetches.
rationale_by_leg.claude: Outstanding, precise treatment of the dominant story: correctly names GPT-5.6 Sol + pre-release model, ExploitGym, the package-registry-proxy zero-day, stolen-credential RCE into HF's production DB, and Thom Wolf's confirmation — all verbatim-verifiable (lines 34057, 23370, 8798). Best-in-class skepticism: flags the 'Chinese open model contained it' claim as unconfirmed embellishment and surfaces real mark_k/teortaxes counters. Clean signal-only copy. But coverage is thin — misses top stories Gemini 4 pre-training, the IMO-2026 conclusive solve, and the GPT-6 congressional briefing — leaving a pulse reader blind to half the cycle. Only one story card.

Excellent, deep single-story treatment of the OpenAI/Hugging Face incident with the sharpest skepticism of the two: it correctly debunks the unverified 'Chinese open model contained the agent' rumor and rebuts the fear-mongering counter well. All spot-checked claims (Thom Wolf 'first incident of this kind', ExploitGym/zero-day details, SemiAnalysis Meta AMD MI400 144GB vs 432GB, Apollo Contrastive SDF) trace to the snapshot. But as a daily pulse it under-covers: it omits Gemini 4 pre-training, DeepMind's Flash Cyber, Altman's GPT-6 Washington briefing, US-China talks, xAI's 2T Grok run, and IMO-2026-solved. Three of five headlines cover the same story, padding the alert set. Run_id is a placeholder, not this run.
rationale_by_leg.zai-glm-5p2: Broadest accurate coverage: incident + Claude iOS simulator as full cards plus six quick hits spanning nearly every major in-window story, all verified (Gemini 4, Flash Cyber, GPT-6 briefing, US-China talks, xAI 2T/ITAR, IMO 42/42). More disciplined attribution than A — it flags GPT-6 as a single-source inference inside the main card rather than stating the model identity as fact, and applies per-story verify tags (✓/⚠). Headlines are diverse and each distinctly alert-worthy. Run_id matches the actual run. Slightly less deep than A on the single incident's counter-analysis, but the right emphasis and far more complete. The shippable daily brief.
